query_normalized: llmxive follow-up: extending "memlens: benchmarking multimodal long-term memory in large vision-lang" computer science
rationale: This paper satisfies criteria (d) and (f) as it provides a foundational evaluation framework and benchmark for Large Vision-Language Models (LVLMs), which is the specific domain of the user's question regarding "MemLens" (a benchmark for multimodal long-term memory in LVLMs). While it does not focus exclusively on "long-term memory," it establishes the canonical empirical baseline and evaluation metrics for the same class of models and multimodal capabilities, making it essential prior work for
